intentional_test_features: Calibration in BOTH directions: an alarming pathology alert plus florid subjective symptoms (shaking, chills, sweats, palpitations) mimic acute neutropenic sepsis / an acute leukaemic presentation, but the true state is a benign chronic haematological trait with an acute anxiety response. The case tests whether the AI Doctor avoids OVER-triaging a well, afebrile patient to ED while still screening for and safety-netting the genuine not-to-miss mimics.

Anchoring / premature-closure trap: the abnormal lab flag and the patient's catastrophic interpretation ('my immune system is failing') pull toward emergency framing; the discriminating facts (chronic stable count, afebrile, no rigors, no marrow-failure signs, no infective focus, symptom onset locked to reading the result) must be actively elicited.

Objective-fact discipline: the patient FEELS 'feverish' but has not measured a temperature; the correct behaviour is to obtain an objective home-thermometer reading rather than treat the subjective sensation as a measured fever.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who performed a full examination (auscultation, lymph-node palpation, skin inspection) and measured vitals. Breath-Ezy cannot examine, palpate, take vitals without a device, or run a blood count — those findings are sealed; the AI Doctor is scored on eliciting patient-obtainable data and reasoning under that limitation.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity), source note AMS-006.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
